Decreasing microleakage is directly linked to improved bond strength between the filling material and the tooth structure due to increased surface area created by acid etching.

raw_response:
Acid etching primarily serves to create a rough surface on teeth, allowing better adhesion of sealants and composite fillings during dental restoration processes. Among the given options:
\boxed{A}
Decreasing microleakage is directly linked to improved bond strength between the filling material and the tooth structure due to increased surface area created by acid etching.
Tooth bonding relies heavily on creating a microporous layer post-acid etching, which enhances resin-dentin interface quality, thus minimizing gaps where bacteria can penetrate leading to microleakage. Therefore, option A is the most advantageous indication of acid etching.
